poets.name: Rajan Rai
poets.bio: Rajan Rai is the founder of Niharika and a contemporary philosopher-poet. His work explores the intersection of human potential, self-realisation, and the quiet power of introspection. Born with a deep love for literature, Rajan has dedicated his life to building a platform where poetry breathes and philosophy speaks.

poets.name: Kabir Das
poets.bio: Kabir Das was a 15th-century Indian mystic poet whose writings influenced Hinduism's Bhakti movement and Sikhism. His verses, known as Dohe, are timeless meditations on truth, love, and the nature of the divine.

poets.name: Harivansh Rai Bachchan
poets.bio: Harivansh Rai Bachchan was one of the most celebrated Hindi poets of the 20th century. His magnum opus Madhushala is considered a masterpiece of Hindi literature.

poets.name: Mirabai
poets.bio: Mirabai was a 16th-century Hindu mystic poet and devotee of Lord Krishna. Her bhajans and poems express an intense, passionate devotion that transcends all boundaries.

poets.name: Ramdhari Singh Dinkar
poets.bio: Ramdhari Singh Dinkar was one of the most important modern Hindi poets. Known for his patriotic and revolutionary poetry, he received the Sahitya Akademi Award and Padma Bhushan.

poets.name: Mahadevi Verma
poets.bio: Mahadevi Verma was a Hindi poet, essayist, and the pillar of Chhayawaad era. Known as the 'Modern Meera', she was awarded the Bharat Ratna for her contribution to Hindi literature.

poets.name: Sumitranandan Pant
poets.bio: Sumitranandan Pant is considered the most lyrical of the Chhayawad poets. Deeply influenced by nature and philosophy, he was awarded the Sahitya Akademi Award, Padma Bhushan, and Jnanpith Award.

poets.name: Jaishankar Prasad
poets.bio: Jaishankar Prasad was a Hindi playwright, novelist, and poet. He is one of the pillars of Chhayavadi poetry. His Kamayani is considered the greatest poem of modern Hindi.

poets.name: Gulzar
poets.bio: Gulzar is an Indian poet, lyricist, and film director. Known for his profound and often symbolic poetry in Hindi and Urdu, he has won countless national and international awards including an Academy Award.

poets.name: Rahim Das
poets.bio: Abdul Rahim Khan-i-Khana, known as Rahim Das, was a medieval Hindi poet and one of the nine gems (Navratnas) in Akbar's court. His dohe are celebrated for their wisdom and humanity.

poets.name: Suryakant Tripathi Nirala
poets.bio: Nirala was a pioneering Hindi poet who introduced free verse (mukt chhand) into Hindi poetry. A rebel by nature, his poetry reflects deep humanity, revolutionary spirit, and lyrical beauty.

poets.name: Dr. Hariom Panwar
poets.bio: Dr. Hariom Panwar is one of the most celebrated contemporary Hindi poets and kavi-sammelan performers. Known for his patriotic and motivational poetry, he has performed across the globe.

poems.text:
A man can do anything,
when he realises he is a man.
poems.hindi:
एक इंसान सब कुछ कर सकता है,
जब वो जान ले कि वो वाकई एक इंसान है।

poems.text:
Silence is the loudest answer
the soul ever gives.
poems.hindi:
खामोशी वो सबसे ऊँची आवाज़ है
जो आत्मा कभी देती है।

poems.text:
Every storm within you
is just a sunrise waiting to break.
poems.hindi:
तुम्हारे भीतर का हर तूफ़ान
बस एक सूर्योदय है जो उगने की राह देख रहा है।

poems.text:
The mind that questions itself
is already wiser than most.
poems.hindi:
वो मन जो खुद से सवाल करता है
वो पहले से ही अधिकांश से अधिक बुद्धिमान है।

poems.text:
Love is not what you feel -
it is what you become.
poems.hindi:
प्रेम वो नहीं जो तुम महसूस करते हो -
प्रेम वो है जो तुम बन जाते हो।

poems.text:
मन रे तन कागद का पुतला,
लागे बूंद बिनसि जाए छिन में।
poems.hindi:
मन रे तन कागद का पुतला,
लागे बूंद बिनसि जाए छिन में।

poems.text:
जो है भार वही है उठाना,
जब तक जियो तब तक यही काम।
poems.hindi:
जो है भार वही है उठाना,
जब तक जियो तब तक यही काम।

poems.text:
मेरे तो गिरिधर गोपाल दूसरो न कोई,
जाके सिर मोर मुकुट मेरो पति सोई।
poems.hindi:
मेरे तो गिरिधर गोपाल दूसरो न कोई,
जाके सिर मोर मुकुट मेरो पति सोई।

poems.text: सिंहासन खाली करो कि जनता आती है।
poems.hindi: सिंहासन खाली करो कि जनता आती है।

poems.text:
रोती है यह पृथ्वी, रोता है यह आकाश,
रोती हैं ये नदियाँ, यह समीर, यह पाश।
poems.hindi:
रोती है यह पृथ्वी, रोता है यह आकाश,
रोती हैं ये नदियाँ, यह समीर, यह पाश।

poems.text:
छोटा मेरा खेत, चौकोना,
कागज़ का एक पन्ना।
poems.hindi:
छोटा मेरा खेत, चौकोना,
कागज़ का एक पन्ना।

poems.text:
रहिमन धागा प्रेम का, मत तोड़ो चटकाय,
टूटे से फिर ना जुड़े, जुड़े गाँठ पड़ जाय।
poems.hindi:
रहिमन धागा प्रेम का, मत तोड़ो चटकाय,
टूटे से फिर ना जुड़े, जुड़े गाँठ पड़ जाय।

quotes.text: A man can do anything, when he realises he is a man.
quotes.hindi: एक इंसान सब कुछ कर सकता है, जब वो जान ले कि वो वाकई एक इंसान है।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: Silence is the loudest answer the soul ever gives.
quotes.hindi: खामोशी वो सबसे ऊँची आवाज़ है जो आत्मा कभी देती है।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: Every storm within you is just a sunrise waiting to break.
quotes.hindi: तुम्हारे भीतर का हर तूफ़ान बस एक सूर्योदय है जो उगने की राह देख रहा है।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: The mind that questions itself is already wiser than most.
quotes.hindi: वो मन जो खुद से सवाल करता है, वो पहले से ही अधिकांश से अधिक बुद्धिमान है।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: Love is not what you feel - it is what you become.
quotes.hindi: प्रेम वो नहीं जो तुम महसूस करते हो - प्रेम वो है जो तुम बन जाते हो।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: The strongest man is not the one who never falls - it is the one who always rises.
quotes.hindi: सबसे मजबूत इंसान वो नहीं जो कभी गिरता नहीं - बल्कि वो है जो हमेशा उठता है।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: Truth does not need many words - it only needs one honest moment.
quotes.hindi: सत्य को कई शब्दों की ज़रूरत नहीं - उसे बस एक ईमानदार पल चाहिए।
quotes.author: Rajan Rai

quotes.text: मन चंगा तो कठौती में गंगा।
quotes.hindi: मन चंगा तो कठौती में गंगा।
quotes.author: Kabir Das

quotes.text: जो पत्थर को भी पिघला दे, वो आग दिल में जलाओ।
quotes.hindi: जो पत्थर को भी पिघला दे, वो आग दिल में जलाओ।
quotes.author: Harivansh Rai Bachchan

kavita.meaning: Poetry; a literary form that expresses emotions, ideas, or narratives through rhythm and language.
kavita.example: उसकी कविता मन को छू लेती है। (Her poetry touches the heart.)

prem.meaning: Love; a deep, unconditional affection or feeling for another person, nature, or the divine.
prem.example: प्रेम सबसे बड़ी शक्ति है। (Love is the greatest power.)

virah.meaning: Separation; the pain of being apart from a loved one - a central theme in Indian poetry.
virah.example: विरह की पीड़ा असहनीय होती है। (The pain of separation is unbearable.)

bhajan.meaning: A devotional song or hymn in praise of God, especially in the Hindu tradition.
bhajan.example: मीराबाई के भजन बहुत प्रसिद्ध हैं। (Mirabai's bhajans are very famous.)

doha.meaning: A traditional Hindi verse form consisting of two rhyming lines, each with 24 syllables. (11+13)
doha.example: कबीर के दोहे आज भी प्रासंगिक हैं। (Kabir's dohas are still relevant today.)

nazm.meaning: A poem, especially one that does not follow a strict verse form - often used for free verse and lyrical poetry.
nazm.example: यह नज़्म बहुत भावुक है। (This poem is very emotional.)

ras.meaning: The essence or flavour of a poem; one of the nine aesthetic emotions (Navarasa) in Indian poetry.
ras.example: श्रृंगार रस प्रेम का प्रतीक है। (Shringar Ras symbolises love.)

alankar.meaning: Figure of speech; a literary device used to enhance the beauty of poetry and prose in Hindi.
alankar.example: उपमा एक प्रमुख अलंकार है। (Simile is a major figure of speech.)

chhand.meaning: Meter or verse; the rhythmic pattern followed in traditional Hindi poetry.
chhand.example: रामायण में दोहा और चौपाई छंद का प्रयोग है। (Ramayan uses Doha and Chaupai meter.)

sahitya.meaning: Literature; the body of written works - poetry, prose, drama - of a language or culture.
sahitya.example: हिंदी साहित्य बहुत समृद्ध है। (Hindi literature is very rich.)

shraddha.meaning: Faith, reverence, deep respect - especially towards a teacher, parent, or spiritual path.
shraddha.example: गुरु के प्रति श्रद्धा होनी चाहिए। (One should have reverence for the teacher.)

jigyasa.meaning: Curiosity, inquisitiveness; the burning desire to know and understand.
jigyasa.example: ज्ञान की जिज्ञासा ही मनुष्य को महान बनाती है। (The curiosity for knowledge makes a person great.)

moksha.meaning: Liberation; freedom from the cycle of birth and death - the highest spiritual goal in Indian philosophy.
moksha.example: मोक्ष की प्राप्ति आत्मज्ञान से होती है। (Liberation is achieved through self-realisation.)

karma.meaning: Action, deed; the principle that one's actions determine one's future - the law of cause and effect.
karma.example: कर्म ही पूजा है। (Action is worship.)

anand.meaning: Joy, bliss, supreme happiness - especially the pure bliss arising from spiritual realisation.
anand.example: भक्ति में आनंद मिलता है। (One finds bliss in devotion.)

blog.title: Understanding the Doha: Kabir's Gift to Hindi Literature
blog.excerpt: The Doha, a two-line verse of 24 syllables, has carried some of the most profound wisdom in Hindi literature. Kabir's dohas continue to resonate across centuries, nations, and religions.
blog.author: Rajan Rai

blog.title: Rajan Rai: Why I Built Niharika for India's Poetry Lovers
blog.excerpt: Niharika was born from a simple belief - that great poetry should be free, accessible, and celebrated. Founder Rajan Rai shares the personal journey behind building India's newest literary home.
blog.author: Rajan Rai

blog.title: Madhushala: Harivansh Rai Bachchan's Timeless Masterpiece
blog.excerpt: Madhushala, written in 1935, uses the metaphor of a tavern and wine to explore life, death, and the divine. 135 quatrains that have never lost their power.
blog.author: Rajan Rai

blog.title: Mirabai: The Saint Who Chose Krishna Over Kingdoms
blog.excerpt: Mirabai defied royalty, social norms, and tradition to follow her burning devotion for Lord Krishna. Her poetry is not just literature - it is a spiritual revolution.
blog.author: Rajan Rai

blog.title: The Nine Rasas: Understanding Emotions in Hindi Poetry
blog.excerpt: Indian aesthetics identifies nine fundamental emotional states - Navarasa - from love (Shringar) to heroism (Veer) to spiritual peace (Shanta). Every great poem vibrates with at least one.
blog.author: Rajan Rai

blog.title: Dinkar's Rashmirathi: The Epic That Reimagined Karna
blog.excerpt: Ramdhari Singh Dinkar's Rashmirathi retells the Mahabharata from Karna's perspective - giving voice to the unrecognised hero, the man born great but denied greatness by destiny.
blog.author: Rajan Rai

videos.title: Kabir Ke Dohe - Complete Collection Reading
Kavi Sammelan - Best of Hindi Poetry 2023

options: A romantic and mystical movement in early 20th century Hindi poetry
